locked_facts: Energy stored in a capacitor: U = ½CV² = ½QV = Q²/2C
Energy density in electric field: u = ½ε₀E²
Energy is stored in the electric field itself, not on the plates
For parallel plate capacitor: U = ½ε₀E²(Ad) where Ad is the volume between plates
Energy density is proportional to the square of the electric field
With dielectric: u = ½Kε₀E² = ½ε₀E₀²/K (field reduced but K compensates)
truth_statements: The energy stored in a capacitor can be expressed as ½CV² or equivalently as Q²/2C or ½QV
The energy per unit volume in an electric field is ½ε₀E² — this shows that energy resides in the field itself
Doubling the electric field quadruples the energy density
The concept of energy density generalises beyond capacitors — any region of space with an electric field contains energy
For a parallel plate capacitor the total energy equals the energy density times the volume between the plates

aha_moment.what_to_show: A capacitor with the space between plates colour-coded by energy density — brighter where E is stronger. Pulling the plates apart (constant Q) shows field staying same but volume increasing, so total energy increases while C decreases.
aha_moment.visual_emphasis: Colour-mapped energy density between plates with total energy bar updating as geometry changes
aha_moment.why_this_breaks_misconception: Students think energy is stored on the plates — seeing the colour fill the SPACE between plates makes the field-energy concept tangible

simulation_states.label: Charged capacitor
simulation_states.description: Parallel plate capacitor with charge Q. Energy bar shows U = ½CV². The space between plates is empty — energy location is ambiguous.
simulation_states.emphasis: Where is the energy actually stored?

simulation_states.label: Energy in the field
simulation_states.description: Electric field lines appear between plates. The space between plates glows with colour intensity proportional to E². Label: u = ½ε₀E². Energy IS the field.
simulation_states.emphasis: Energy density u = ½ε₀E² — the energy lives in the field, not on the plates

simulation_states.label: Geometry changes
simulation_states.description: Plates pulled apart at constant Q. Field stays same (E = σ/ε₀ unchanged), but volume increases → total U increases. C decreases (C = ε₀A/d). Energy bar grows.
simulation_states.emphasis: Same field, more volume → more total energy. U = Q²/2C increases as C drops.

simulation_states.label: Interactive
simulation_states.description: Student adjusts plate separation and charge. Energy density colour map updates. Graph shows u vs E (parabola). Toggle between constant-Q and constant-V modes.
simulation_states.emphasis: Watch how energy density scales as E² — double the field, quadruple the energy

common_misconceptions.belief: Energy is stored on the capacitor plates
common_misconceptions.correction: Energy is stored in the electric field between the plates. The energy density u = ½ε₀E² shows that wherever there is an electric field, there is stored energy.
common_misconceptions.simulation_emphasis: Colour the space between plates to show energy density — plates themselves are dark

common_misconceptions.belief: Pulling plates apart at constant charge doesn't change energy
common_misconceptions.correction: At constant Q, pulling plates apart increases energy (U = Q²/2C, and C decreases). The extra energy comes from the work done in pulling the plates apart against the attractive force.
common_misconceptions.simulation_emphasis: Animate plates separating with energy bar increasing and work arrow shown
